trans-unit.source: Geodata Selection and Queries
trans-unit.target: Geodata Selection and Queries

trans-unit.source: Geodata selection and queries in QGIS allow targeted parts of geospatial datasets to be selected. This can be utilized for filtering and analyzing geospatial information, which are essential tasks when working with spatial data. \ \ Three general types of selection/queries in QGIS that will cover the majority of your needs include:\ **1. Manual Selection:** Selection by manually choosing features with one of various selection tools offered by QGIS.
trans-unit.target: Geodata selection and queries in QGIS allow targeted parts of geospatial datasets to be selected. This can be utilized for filtering and analyzing geospatial information, which are essential tasks when working with spatial data. \ \ Three general types of selection/queries in QGIS that will cover the majority of your needs include:\ **1. Manual Selection:** Selection by manually choosing features with one of various selection tools offered by QGIS.

trans-unit.source: **2. Attribute based Selection:** Selection based on attribute values stored in the attribute table.
trans-unit.target: **2. Attribute based Selection:** Selection based on attribute values stored in the attribute table.

trans-unit.source: **3. Layer based spatial Selection:** Selecting features in one layer based on specified geometric relationships with features in another layer.
trans-unit.target: **3. Layer based spatial Selection:** Selecting features in one layer based on specified geometric relationships with features in another layer.

trans-unit.source: ::::{admonition} Now it's your turn!
trans-unit.target: ::::{admonition} Now it's your turn!

trans-unit.source: Querying data is essential to understanding and manipulating your datasets. You can follow the steps outlined below by downloading [this dataset](https://datacatalog.worldbank.org/search/dataset/0038272/World-Bank-Official-Boundaries).
trans-unit.target: Querying data is essential to understanding and manipulating your datasets. You can follow the steps outlined below by downloading [this dataset](https://datacatalog.worldbank.org/search/dataset/0038272/World-Bank-Official-Boundaries).

trans-unit.source: __Download World Bank Official Boundaries__
trans-unit.target: __Download World Bank Official Boundaries__

trans-unit.source: Manual Selection
trans-unit.target: Manual Selection

trans-unit.source: Manual selection is done mostly using one of the click-based selection tools available under in your project toolbar (alternative: `Edit` → `Select`). These include `Select Feature(s)`, `Select Feature by Polygon`, `Select Feature by Freehand` and `Select Feature by Radius`. \ \ Example: `Select Feature(s)`
trans-unit.target: Manual selection is done mostly using one of the click-based selection tools available under in your project toolbar (alternative: `Edit` → `Select`). These include `Select Feature(s)`, `Select Feature by Polygon`, `Select Feature by Freehand` and `Select Feature by Radius`. \ \ Example: `Select Feature(s)`

trans-unit.source: Click `Select Feature(s)` in the drop down menu of ![](/fig/mActionSelectRectangle.png).
trans-unit.target: Click `Select Feature(s)` in the drop down menu of ![](/fig/mActionSelectRectangle.png).

trans-unit.source: Select features by clicking on the or drawing a rectangle overlapping them.
trans-unit.target: Select features by clicking on the or drawing a rectangle overlapping them.

trans-unit.source: Use the tool outside of selectable features to end your selection.
trans-unit.target: Use the tool outside of selectable features to end your selection.

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} Example: Manually select country polygons by click :open:
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} Example: Manually select country polygons by click :open:

trans-unit.source: The other options of ![](/fig/mActionSelectRectangle.png) function in a similar way, selecting all features overlapping with the respective geometry generated by the tools.
trans-unit.target: The other options of ![](/fig/mActionSelectRectangle.png) function in a similar way, selecting all features overlapping with the respective geometry generated by the tools.

trans-unit.source: Click `Select Feature(s) by Polygon` in the drop down menu of ![](/fig/mActionSelectRectangle.png).
trans-unit.target: Click `Select Feature(s) by Polygon` in the drop down menu of ![](/fig/mActionSelectRectangle.png).

trans-unit.source: Select features by left clicking around the features you want to select.
trans-unit.target: Select features by left clicking around the features you want to select.

trans-unit.source: Right-click when you are done drawing the polygon.
trans-unit.target: Right-click when you are done drawing the polygon.

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} Example: Manually select countries by drawing a polygon :open:
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} Example: Manually select countries by drawing a polygon :open:

trans-unit.source: Attribute Based Selection
trans-unit.target: Attribute Based Selection

trans-unit.source: A query based on specific Attributes can be done utilizing the `Select Features by Expression` tool available under ![](/fig/mActionSelectbyExpression.png) in the project toolbar and the attribute table (alternative: `Edit` → `Select` → `Select` Features by Expression).
trans-unit.target: A query based on specific Attributes can be done utilizing the `Select Features by Expression` tool available under ![](/fig/mActionSelectbyExpression.png) in the project toolbar and the attribute table (alternative: `Edit` → `Select` → `Select` Features by Expression).

trans-unit.source: In the tool interface, expand `Fields and Values` in the right hand panel.
trans-unit.target: In the tool interface, expand `Fields and Values` in the right hand panel.

trans-unit.source: Choose the field you want to base your selection on by double clicking it (it should now appear in the expression panel on the left-hand side).
trans-unit.target: Choose the field you want to base your selection on by double clicking it (it should now appear in the expression panel on the left-hand side).

trans-unit.source: Use an expression with specific operators to specify your selection in the left hand panel (e.g `"continent" LIKE 'Asia'` to select all features with the value “Asia” in the field "continent" ).
trans-unit.target: Use an expression with specific operators to specify your selection in the left hand panel (e.g `"continent" LIKE 'Asia'` to select all features with the value “Asia” in the field "continent" ).

trans-unit.source: :::{tab-item} Comparison operators | operator | functionality | |----------|--------------------------| | __=__ | equals | | __!=__ | not equal | | __<__ | less than | | __>__ | greater than | | __<=__ | less than or equal to | | __>=__ | greater than or equal to | :::
trans-unit.target: :::{tab-item} Comparison operators | operator | functionality | |----------|--------------------------| | __=__ | equals | | __!=__ | not equal | | __<__ | less than | | __>__ | greater than | | __<=__ | less than or equal to | | __>=__ | greater than or equal to | :::

trans-unit.source: :::{tab-item} Logical operators Operators such as AND, OR can be used to combine different queries or criteria. | operator | functionality | |----------|------------------------| | __AND__ | logical AND | | __OR__ | logical OR | | __NOT__ | logical NOT | :::
trans-unit.target: :::{tab-item} Logical operators Operators such as AND, OR can be used to combine different queries or criteria. | operator | functionality | |----------|------------------------| | __AND__ | logical AND | | __OR__ | logical OR | | __NOT__ | logical NOT | :::

trans-unit.source: :::{tab-item} Special operators | operator | functionality | |---------------|------------------------------------------------| | __LIKE__ | pattern matching | | __IN__ | checks if a value is in a list of values | | __IS NULL__ | checks for null values | | __BETWEEN__ | checks if a value is within a specified range | | __CASE WHEN__ | conditional expressions | :::
trans-unit.target: :::{tab-item} Special operators | operator | functionality | |---------------|------------------------------------------------| | __LIKE__ | pattern matching | | __IN__ | checks if a value is in a list of values | | __IS NULL__ | checks for null values | | __BETWEEN__ | checks if a value is within a specified range | | __CASE WHEN__ | conditional expressions | :::

trans-unit.source:
:::{dropdown} Example: Select all country polygons that share the value `Asia` in the field "continent" :open:
:::
trans-unit.target:
:::{dropdown} Example: Select all country polygons that share the value `Asia` in the field "continent" :open:
:::

trans-unit.source: Layer Based Spatial Selection
trans-unit.target: Layer Based Spatial Selection

trans-unit.source: Spatial selection of features enables selecting parts of a layer based on their relationship with features in another geospatial layer (e.g. the selection of all point features in layer A that are located within a polygon feature in Layer B). It can be achieved by using the tool “Select by Location” available under ![](/fig/mActionSelectbyLocation.png) in the project toolbar or (alternative: `Vector` → `Research Tools` → `Select by Location`).
trans-unit.target: Spatial selection of features enables selecting parts of a layer based on their relationship with features in another geospatial layer (e.g. the selection of all point features in layer A that are located within a polygon feature in Layer B). It can be achieved by using the tool “Select by Location” available under ![](/fig/mActionSelectbyLocation.png) in the project toolbar or (alternative: `Vector` → `Research Tools` → `Select by Location`).

trans-unit.source: In the tool interface, choose the vector layer you want to select features from using `Select features from` and the layer you want to base the selection on using `By comparing to the features from`.
trans-unit.target: In the tool interface, choose the vector layer you want to select features from using `Select features from` and the layer you want to base the selection on using `By comparing to the features from`.

trans-unit.source: Choose the geometric operator that will be used to select features (see bottom paragraph).
trans-unit.target: Choose the geometric operator that will be used to select features (see bottom paragraph).

trans-unit.source: In the bottom prompt, choose how you want to proceed with the newly selected features. The options include:
trans-unit.target: In the bottom prompt, choose how you want to proceed with the newly selected features. The options include:

trans-unit.source: Creating a new selection.
trans-unit.target: Creating a new selection.

trans-unit.source: Adding to the current selection.
trans-unit.target: Adding to the current selection.

trans-unit.source: Selecting within the current selection.
trans-unit.target: Selecting within the current selection.

trans-unit.source: Removing from the current selection.
trans-unit.target: Removing from the current selection.

trans-unit.source: Selected features are again highlighted in bright yellow in your geospatial interface.
trans-unit.target: Selected features are again highlighted in bright yellow in your geospatial interface.

trans-unit.source:
:::{dropdown} Example: Select Cities (Point features) that intersect with the polygon "China" :open:
:::
trans-unit.target:
:::{dropdown} Example: Select Cities (Point features) that intersect with the polygon "China" :open:
:::

trans-unit.source:
:::{dropdown} Example 2: Select all country polygons that touch the polygon "China" :open:
:::
trans-unit.target:
:::{dropdown} Example 2: Select all country polygons that touch the polygon "China" :open:
:::

trans-unit.source: Geometric Operators
trans-unit.target: Geometric Operators

trans-unit.source: The geometric operators define the conditions of the relationship between your source and target layer that the selection will be based on. There are eight total options:
trans-unit.target: The geometric operators define the conditions of the relationship between your source and target layer that the selection will be based on. There are eight total options:

trans-unit.source: Operation
trans-unit.target: Operation

trans-unit.source: Description
trans-unit.target: Description

trans-unit.source: Example
trans-unit.target: Example

trans-unit.source: Intersect
trans-unit.target: Intersect

trans-unit.source: Features from the target layer are selected if they intersect with any features in the source layer.
trans-unit.target: Features from the target layer are selected if they intersect with any features in the source layer.

trans-unit.source: Selecting all roads that cross a polygon representing a national park.
trans-unit.target: Selecting all roads that cross a polygon representing a national park.

trans-unit.source: Contain
trans-unit.target: Contain

trans-unit.source: Features from the target layer are selected if they completely contain features in the source layer.
trans-unit.target: Features from the target layer are selected if they completely contain features in the source layer.

trans-unit.source: Selecting a country polygon that completely contains smaller polygons of cities.
trans-unit.target: Selecting a country polygon that completely contains smaller polygons of cities.

trans-unit.source: Disjoint
trans-unit.target: Disjoint

trans-unit.source: Features are disjoint if they do not share any common point or area.
trans-unit.target: Features are disjoint if they do not share any common point or area.

trans-unit.source: Selecting administrative districts that have no common boundary or area.
trans-unit.target: Selecting administrative districts that have no common boundary or area.

trans-unit.source: Equal
trans-unit.target: Equal

trans-unit.source: Features are equal if their geometries are identical.
trans-unit.target: Features are equal if their geometries are identical.

trans-unit.source: Selecting two line segments with the exact same set of coordinates.
trans-unit.target: Selecting two line segments with the exact same set of coordinates.

trans-unit.source: Touch
trans-unit.target: Touch

trans-unit.source: Features from the target layer are selected if they touch features in the source layer.
trans-unit.target: Features from the target layer are selected if they touch features in the source layer.

trans-unit.source: Selecting parks that touch a specific road.
trans-unit.target: Selecting parks that touch a specific road.

trans-unit.source: Overlap
trans-unit.target: Overlap

trans-unit.source: Features from the target layer are selected if they share any common space with features in the source layer.
trans-unit.target: Features from the target layer are selected if they share any common space with features in the source layer.

trans-unit.source: Selecting land parcels that overlap with a proposed construction zone.
trans-unit.target: Selecting land parcels that overlap with a proposed construction zone.

trans-unit.source: Are within
trans-unit.target: Are within

trans-unit.source: Features from the target layer are selected if they are completely within features in the source layer.
trans-unit.target: Features from the target layer are selected if they are completely within features in the source layer.

trans-unit.source: Selecting building polygons that are entirely within a polygon of a city boundary.
trans-unit.target: Selecting building polygons that are entirely within a polygon of a city boundary.

trans-unit.source: Cross
trans-unit.target: Cross

trans-unit.source: Features from the target layer are selected if they cross features in the source layer.
trans-unit.target: Features from the target layer are selected if they cross features in the source layer.

trans-unit.source: Selecting rivers that cross a road.
trans-unit.target: Selecting rivers that cross a road.

trans-unit.source: Self-Assessment Questions
trans-unit.target: Self-Assessment Questions

trans-unit.source: ::::{admonition} Test your knowledge :class: note
trans-unit.target: ::::{admonition} Test your knowledge :class: note

trans-unit.source: __What are the three general types of selection or queries in QGIS? Give a brief description of each.__
trans-unit.target: __What are the three general types of selection or queries in QGIS? Give a brief description of each.__

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} Answer
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} Answer

trans-unit.source: __Manual selection__: You select features with your mouse using the `Select Features`-tool in the map canvas or by clicking on the row column in the attribute table (the numbers on the left of the attribute table). In the map canvas, selected features will be highlighted in bright yellow, in the attribute table they are highlighted in blue.
trans-unit.target: __Manual selection__: You select features with your mouse using the `Select Features`-tool in the map canvas or by clicking on the row column in the attribute table (the numbers on the left of the attribute table). In the map canvas, selected features will be highlighted in bright yellow, in the attribute table they are highlighted in blue.

trans-unit.source: __Attribute-based queries__ (Select by Attribute or Select by Expression): You build a query selecting attributes with specific values (e.g. `"population" > 10000 AND "type" = 'urban'`).
trans-unit.target: __Attribute-based queries__ (Select by Attribute or Select by Expression): You build a query selecting attributes with specific values (e.g. `"population" > 10000 AND "type" = 'urban'`).

trans-unit.source: __Spatial queries__ (Select by Location): You select features based on their spatial relationship to features in another layer (e.g. those that intersect, touch or are disjoint) :::
trans-unit.target: __Spatial queries__ (Select by Location): You select features based on their spatial relationship to features in another layer (e.g. those that intersect, touch or are disjoint) :::

trans-unit.source: __Name at least four geometric operators in spatial queries (e.g. “Intersect”, “Within”) and explain what they do.__
trans-unit.target: __Name at least four geometric operators in spatial queries (e.g. “Intersect”, “Within”) and explain what they do.__

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} Answer
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} Answer

trans-unit.source: Operator
trans-unit.target: Operator

trans-unit.source: Meaning / Behavior
trans-unit.target: Meaning / Behavior

trans-unit.source: **Intersect**
trans-unit.target: **Intersect**

trans-unit.source: Selects features from layer A that intersect (share any part) with features from layer B (they overlap in any way)
trans-unit.target: Selects features from layer A that intersect (share any part) with features from layer B (they overlap in any way)

trans-unit.source: **Within**
trans-unit.target: **Within**

trans-unit.source: Selects features of A that lie completely inside features of B
trans-unit.target: Selects features of A that lie completely inside features of B

trans-unit.source: **Contains**
trans-unit.target: **Contains**

trans-unit.source: Selects features of A that entirely contain features of B
trans-unit.target: Selects features of A that entirely contain features of B

trans-unit.source: **Touches**
trans-unit.target: **Touches**

trans-unit.source: Selects features of A that touch (share a boundary or point) with features of B but do not overlap internally
trans-unit.target: Selects features of A that touch (share a boundary or point) with features of B but do not overlap internally

trans-unit.source: **Crosses**
trans-unit.target: **Crosses**

trans-unit.source: Selects features of A that pass through features of B (they cross each other)
trans-unit.target: Selects features of A that pass through features of B (they cross each other)

trans-unit.source: **Overlaps**
trans-unit.target: **Overlaps**

trans-unit.source: Selects features of A and B that overlap but neither is completely inside the other
trans-unit.target: Selects features of A and B that overlap but neither is completely inside the other

trans-unit.source: As examples:
trans-unit.target: As examples:

trans-unit.source: If you want all roads that cross a park boundary, use Intersect or Crosses
trans-unit.target: If you want all roads that cross a park boundary, use Intersect or Crosses

trans-unit.source: If you want all building footprints entirely inside a flood zone, use Within.
trans-unit.target: If you want all building footprints entirely inside a flood zone, use Within.
